channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: With doors closed to the US, asylum-seekers turn to their Plan B: A new life in Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — When Angelica Delgado took a one-way flight to Mexico as she fled Cuba in December, she was set on seeking asylum in the United States. But after President Donald Trump effectively slammed the door on asylum-seekers crossing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Modi is meeting with Trump in a visit meant to boost the US-India relationship and avoid tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump is meeting with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, who has heaped praise on the president and is hoping to avoid tariffs that the new administration has slapped on other countries in its opening weeks.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel threatens 'all hell will break loose' on Hamas in latest Gaza ceasefire crisis
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense minister on Wednesday vowed that “all hell will break loose” on Hamas if it fails to free hostages this weekend as planned, stepping up threats against the militant group as mediators worked to salvage their ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Seoul says North Korea is destroying facility that hosted reunions of war-separated families
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea is demolishing a South Korea-built property that had been used to host reunions of families separated during the 1950-53 Korean War, the South’s government said Thursday, as it continues to eliminate symbols of engagement between the war-divided rivals.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A joke petition seeks to help Denmark buy California as Trump eyes Greenland
item.description: Is California for sale? A lighthearted petition to buy the state of California for Denmark prompted more than 200,000 signatures by Wednesday in response to President Donald Trump’s talk about taking control of Greenland, a vast and mineral-rich Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump upends US policy on Ukraine and says he and Putin have agreed to begin talks on ending the war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump upended three years of U.S. policy toward Ukraine on Wednesday, saying that he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin had agreed to begin negotiations on ending the war following a sudden prisoner swap.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Congolese church leaders meet Rwanda-backed rebels in the east in latest push for peace
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Congo’s largest church organizations met Wednesday with Rwanda-backed rebels in the country’s east in the latest push for peace and dialogue after weekslong fighting that saw the rebels seize the region’s largest city of Goma.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At least 10 hospitalized in fire at Rio de Janeiro's Carnival costume factory
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A fire Wednesday at a Rio de Janeiro factory that produces costumes for the city's iconic Carnival left 10 people hospitalized, Rio’s state health department said, casting a shadow over preparations for one of the world’s biggest shows.
item.dc:creator: DIARLEI RODRIGUES and ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard sworn in as top intelligence chief in Oval Office
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard was sworn in as director of national intelligence on Wednesday in the Oval Office, hours after the Senate confirmed her. Attorney General Pam Bondi administered the oath of office to Gabbard, who Trump called “an American of extraordinary courage and patriotism.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Things to know about Russia's release of American Marc Fogel and the impact on ending Ukraine war
item.description: The U.S. and Russia agreed to a prisoner swap involving Marc Fogel, an American schoolteacher who the Biden administration had deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, in a diplomatic move that the White House said could move forward negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says American among three freed in Belarus
item.description: The White House said Wednesday that President Trump secured the release of at least three people jailed in Belarus, including an American it has not named and a Belarusian journalist.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Thousands in Taiwan and China celebrate the Lantern Festival with high hopes and rice dumplings
item.description: Thousands in Taiwan and China have celebrated the Lantern Festival, a holiday that marks the end of the Lunar New Year period and is celebrated on the 15th day of the first month of the lunar calendar.
item.dc:creator: Simina Mistreanu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jailed Russian released in US in exchange for Marc Fogel: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said early Wednesday that American Marc Fogel's release came in conjunction with the Trump administration granting freedom to a Russian citizen held in a U.S. prison — though they were not identified.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: UN rights office estimates up to 1,400 killed in crackdown on protests in Bangladesh
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. human rights office on Wednesday estimated that up to 1,400 people may have been killed in Bangladesh over three weeks last summer in a crackdown on student-led protests against the now-ousted former prime minister.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Relatives of Israeli hostages are terrified as the Gaza ceasefire teeters
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — After 16 months of excruciating uncertainty, Idit Ohel finally received word this week that her 24-year-old son, a hostage in Gaza, is still alive.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. This is what you can do to lower your risk
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Everyone loves a breath of fresh air. Unfortunately, too often our air is anything but fresh.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze sets back fight against human trafficking in Cambodia
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — President Donald Trump’s freeze on foreign assistance has dealt a blow to organizations fighting human trafficking and forced labor in Cambodia, where tens of thousands of people are held captive and forced to work in call centers running telephone scams.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel’s fatal shooting of a pregnant Palestinian woman puts the focus on West Bank violence
item.description: KAFR AL-LABAD, West Bank (AP) — The call came in the middle of the night, Mohammed Shula said. His daughter-in-law, eight months pregnant with her first child, was whispering. There was panic in her voice. “Help, please,” Shula recalled her saying. “You have to save us.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli cabinet backs Trump’s demand for Hamas to release all hostages by Saturday deadline
item.description: Israel’s security cabinet supports President Trump’s demand that Hamas release all of its hostages by noon on Saturday, an Israeli official told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump remains committed to US owning Gaza, says Middle East states could help rebuild war-torn area
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is committed to the U.S. purchasing and owning Gaza, but that he may allow Middle Eastern countries to rebuild sections of the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Days before Trump halted funding, an ex-Israeli hostage was held at UNRWA school in Gaza, she reveals
item.description: Former Israeli hostage Emily Damari said she was held in an UNRWA center in Gaza. Damari was recently released as part of the hostages for ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defaced Holocaust mural finds new home in Rome's Shoah Museum
item.description: Contemporary pop artist aleXsandro Palombo is speaking out after his murals showing Holocaust survivors were defaced multiple times in Italy in apparent acts of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
